dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7716 IH: Tariff Free Farming Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Tokuda (for herself, Mrs. McClain Delaney, Mrs. Hayes, Ms. Salinas, Mr. Figures, Ms. Stansbury, Ms. Morrison, and Ms. Kelly of Illinois) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To prohibit the imposition of additional tariffs on agricultural inputs imported from countries to which the United States has extended normal trade relations, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Tariff Free Farming Act.

section.header: Normal trade relations tariffs on agricultural inputs

subsection.header: Maintenance of tariff rate for agricultural inputs
subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law or regulation imposing tariffs on a country-by-country basis, including any authority with respect to the imposition of tariffs under emergency situations, no tariff or other duty may be imposed in excess of the rate assessed as of January 19, 2025, with respect to any article classifiable as an article described in subsection (b) that is imported to the United States from a country to which the United States has extended normal trade relations.
An article described in this subsection is any of the following:

paragraph.text: Seed, including corn, soybean, and wheat.
Crop protection chemicals, including herbicides, insecticides, fungicides.
Fuel and energy articles, including diesel, propane, electricity.
Machinery and equipment, including tractors, combines, replacement parts, specialty crop harvesters.
Building materials, including steel, lumber, fencing.
Other inputs, including supplies and components necessary for farm production.
